name: deflate
documentation:
# deflate API

This is a very thin Python wrapper Eric Biggers' excellent
[libdeflate](https://github.com/ebiggers/libdeflate).

Currently, it handles:

## Compression and decompression of gzip data, with a very basic API

```python
import deflate
level = 6  # The default; may be 1-12 for libdeflate.
compressed = deflate.gzip_compress(b"hello world!" * 1000, level)
original = deflate.gzip_decompress(compressed)
```

## Compression and decompression of raw DEFLATE or zlib data

The original size of the decompressed data needs to be kept through additional logic.

```python
import deflate
level = 6  # The default; may be 1-12 for libdeflate.
data = b"hello world!" * 1000
# DEFLATE
compressed = deflate.deflate_compress(data, level)
original = deflate.deflate_decompress(compressed, len(data))
# zlib
compressed = deflate.zlib_compress(data, level)
original = deflate.zlib_decompress(compressed, len(data))
```

## CRC32 computation

```python
import deflate
crc32 = deflate.crc32(b"hello world! ")  # initial
crc32 = deflate.crc32(b"hello universe!", crc32)  # continued
```

## Adler-32 computation

```python
import deflate
adler32 = deflate.adler32(b"hello world! ")  # initial
adler32 = deflate.adler32(b"hello universe!", adler32)  # continued
```

# Installation

`pip install deflate`

By default, `deflate` will compile and statically link the bundled `libdeflate` when you
build from source. To link to a system-installed `libdeflate`, set the
`LIBDEFLATE_PREFIX` environment variable:

```
LIBDEFLATE_PREFIX=/opt/homebrew/Cellar/libdeflate/1.20 python -m build
```

# Testing

```
pip install -r requirements-dev.lock
python -m pytest
```

modules.name: tests.test_crc32
documentation: test crc32 function

functions.name: tests.test_crc32.test_crc32_simple
documentation: test whether crc32 function computes correctly (reference: zlib.crc32)

functions.name: tests.test_crc32.test_crc32_with_start_value
documentation:
test whether crc32 function computes correctly,
with start value (reference: zlib.crc32)

modules.name: tests.test_adler32
documentation: test adler32 function

functions.name: tests.test_adler32.test_adler_simple
documentation: test whether adler32 function computes correctly (reference: zlib.adler32)

functions.name: tests.test_adler32.test_adler32_with_start_value
documentation:
test whether adler32 function computes correctly,
with start value (reference: zlib.adler32)

modules.name: libdeflate.scripts.gen_bitreverse_tab
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python3
This script computes a table that maps each byte to its bitwise reverse.

modules.name: tests.test_compress
documentation: test compress / decompress functionality

functions.name: tests.test_compress.test_roundtrip
documentation: test whether decompressing compressed data yields the original data

functions.name: tests.test_compress.test_array
documentation: test roundtripping compressed array.array values

functions.name: tests.test_compress.test_shorter
documentation:
tests whether compressed data is actually shorter than original data and
also uses all supported compresslevels

functions.name: tests.test_compress.test_unsupported_compresslevel
documentation: test if compresslevels outside of supported range raise ValueError

functions.name: tests.test_compress.test_invalid_decompression
documentation: test whether giving invalid data to decompress raises ValueError

functions.name: tests.test_compress.test_decompress_resize
documentation:
test that decompressing with overestimated originalsize works, and fails when
originalsize is too small

modules.name: libdeflate.scripts.gen_offset_slot_map
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python3
This script generates the deflate_offset_slot[] array, which maps
'offset - 1 => offset_slot' for offset <= 256.

modules.name: libdeflate.scripts.gen_default_litlen_costs
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python3
This script computes the default litlen symbol costs for the near-optimal
parser.

variables.name: libdeflate.scripts.gen_default_litlen_costs.BIT_COST
documentation: Must match BIT_COST in deflate_compress.c
